meta.subtitle: A business-style static dashboard that explains both the market outcomes and the pipeline behind them.

sources.title: ABS Livestock Products, Australia
sources.scope: Production releases used: 2025-12 to 2025-12. Each workbook contains long historical quarterly series.
logic: Read the Data1 worksheet from each workbook.
Extract description, series type, and series id metadata from fixed ABS rows.
Keep only Original series to avoid mixing adjusted or trend variants.
Deduplicate overlapping releases by keeping the latest release_month for each date + series_id record.
Filter the final business window down to 2020-01 through 2025-12.

sources.title: DAFF Monthly 57 Destination Reports
logic: Read the Report worksheet and normalize destination column names.
Keep only the dashboard metrics: Beef & Veal Total, Total Lamb, Total Mutton, and Total Meats.
Exclude Total Aus and destination subtotal rows such as Total Asia or Other EU to prevent double counting.
Deduplicate overlapping release folders by keeping the latest release_month per report_month + destination + metric_name.
Aggregate monthly flows into quarterly export totals for report-ready views.

pipeline.summary: The dashboard is driven by a repeatable ETL flow that starts from raw Excel files, standardizes market measures, removes duplicate time series, validates coverage, and packages business-ready outputs for static delivery.

nodes.title: Raw Source Intake
logic: Organize source files by release folder under data/raw/production and data/raw/exports.
Treat each folder as a release event rather than as final business truth.
nodes.why: This preserves lineage and lets the pipeline compare overlapping releases safely.

nodes.title: Production Cleaning
logic: Melt wide quarterly tables into long fact rows.
Parse measure, animal, and state from ABS description strings.
Keep Original series only.
Keep the latest release for each date + series_id pair.
Restrict the business window to 2020-01 through 2025-12.
nodes.why: Production workbooks contain decades of repeated history, so release-aware deduplication is essential.

nodes.title: Export Cleaning
logic: Normalize headers and destination names.
Keep monthly flow measures for beef, lamb, mutton, and total meats.
Remove Total Aus and subtotal destinations such as Total Asia or Other Middle East.
Keep the latest release for each report_month + destination + metric_name.
nodes.why: Destination subtotals would otherwise inflate totals and distort ranking views.

nodes.title: Quarterly Reporting Layer
logic: Aggregate monthly export flows into quarterly destination totals.
Merge quarterly exports with Australia-level production and slaughter signals.
Create a compact market summary table for beef and lamb.
nodes.why: This layer powers both executive summary views and chart-ready comparisons.

nodes.title: Static Report Packaging
logic: Render six report figures as reusable PNG assets.
Export chart series and narrative metadata into dashboard_data.json.
Load everything into a static front-end suitable for GitHub Pages.
nodes.why: The final business layer is portable, reviewable, and ready for public portfolio presentation.

businessReport.title: 2026 Commercial Outlook
businessReport.subtitle: Integrated business report for 2020-01 to 2025-12, combining market insights, forecast scenarios, and operating risk factors.
executiveSummary: Beef is the clearer growth engine, with export expansion outpacing production growth in the latest year-on-year view.
Lamb needs portfolio-level interpretation: total exports softened, but export share of production stayed elevated because supply also declined.
Destination exposure is a central commercial risk, especially for beef where the top four markets account for most export volume.
The base forecast points to 1,510,923 tonnes of beef exports and 367,201 tonnes of lamb exports in 2026.
Base export-share pressure is estimated at 52.6% for beef and 56.2% for lamb in 2026.

keyFindings.title: Beef | Export growth
keyFindings.businessSignal: Beef export demand changed 15.4% from 2024 to 2025.
keyFindings.recommendation: Prioritise capacity planning and destination monitoring if growth is above production growth.
keyFindings.narrative: Beef exports reached 1,519,984 tonnes in 2025, a 15.4% change versus 2024.

keyFindings.title: Beef | Production growth
keyFindings.recommendation: Use production growth as a supply-side anchor when interpreting export momentum.
keyFindings.narrative: Beef production was 2,869,097 tonnes in 2025, compared with 2,569,121 tonnes in 2024.

keyFindings.title: Beef | Export share of production
keyFindings.businessSignal: Beef exports represented 53.0% of production in 2025.
keyFindings.recommendation: Track this ratio as a pressure indicator: a rising share means more output is being pulled into export channels.
keyFindings.narrative: Beef export share of production moved from 51.2% in 2024 to 53.0% in 2025.

keyFindings.title: Lamb | Export growth
keyFindings.businessSignal: Lamb export demand changed -2.5% from 2024 to 2025.
keyFindings.recommendation: Investigate destination-level weakness before treating the decline as a category-wide issue.
keyFindings.narrative: Lamb exports reached 336,724 tonnes in 2025, a -2.5% change versus 2024.

keyFindings.title: Lamb | Production growth
keyFindings.recommendation: Use production growth as a supply-side anchor when interpreting export momentum.
keyFindings.narrative: Lamb production was 597,068 tonnes in 2025, compared with 629,682 tonnes in 2024.

keyFindings.title: Lamb | Export share of production
keyFindings.businessSignal: Lamb exports represented 56.4% of production in 2025.
keyFindings.recommendation: Track this ratio as a pressure indicator: a rising share means more output is being pulled into export channels.
keyFindings.narrative: Lamb export share of production moved from 54.8% in 2024 to 56.4% in 2025.

keyFindings.title: Beef | Destination concentration
keyFindings.businessSignal: The top four Beef destinations represented 73.5% of 2025 export volume.
keyFindings.recommendation: Treat the top destinations as priority account markets, while monitoring concentration risk.
keyFindings.narrative: Beef export demand was concentrated in USA East, China, Japan, South Korea, which together accounted for 73.5% of 2025 export volume.

keyFindings.title: Lamb | Destination concentration
keyFindings.businessSignal: The top four Lamb destinations represented 52.3% of 2025 export volume.
keyFindings.recommendation: Treat the top destinations as priority account markets, while monitoring concentration risk.
keyFindings.narrative: Lamb export demand was concentrated in USA East, China, Dubai, South Korea, which together accounted for 52.3% of 2025 export volume.

keyFindings.title: Beef | Destination gain
keyFindings.businessSignal: China added 79,711 tonnes of Beef export volume versus 2024.
keyFindings.recommendation: Use high-growth destinations to explain the demand story and identify priority markets.
keyFindings.narrative: China was a major Beef growth destination in 2025, rising 41.3% year on year.

keyFindings.title: Beef | Destination gain
keyFindings.businessSignal: USA East added 59,178 tonnes of Beef export volume versus 2024.
keyFindings.recommendation: Use high-growth destinations to explain the demand story and identify priority markets.
keyFindings.narrative: USA East was a major Beef growth destination in 2025, rising 19.4% year on year.

keyFindings.title: Beef | Destination gain
keyFindings.businessSignal: South Korea added 20,805 tonnes of Beef export volume versus 2024.
keyFindings.recommendation: Use high-growth destinations to explain the demand story and identify priority markets.
keyFindings.narrative: South Korea was a major Beef growth destination in 2025, rising 10.4% year on year.

keyFindings.title: Beef | Destination decline
keyFindings.businessSignal: Indonesia reduced Beef export volume by 17,596 tonnes versus 2024.
keyFindings.recommendation: Flag declining destinations as a portfolio risk or a shift in market allocation.
keyFindings.narrative: Indonesia was a material drag on Beef exports in 2025, changing -20.9% year on year.

forecast.model: Linear trend with monthly or quarterly seasonality
forecast.methodNote: The forecast is a transparent baseline model. Scenario bands are not live AI predictions; they translate model error and business uncertainty into conservative, base, and high cases.

impactFactors.businessImpact: Rainfall, pasture availability, heat stress, and water constraints can change herd or flock turnoff, carcass weights, and production timing.
impactFactors.forecastUse: Use the conservative production case when seasonal conditions reduce supply confidence.

impactFactors.businessImpact: Feed, energy, and on-farm operating costs influence finishing decisions and can shift the volume available for export channels.
impactFactors.forecastUse: Stress test the base case if input costs rise faster than export prices.

impactFactors.businessImpact: Labour availability, plant throughput, refrigeration, and container capacity can limit how quickly production converts into export shipments.
impactFactors.forecastUse: Treat high export scenarios as capacity-dependent rather than purely demand-driven.

impactFactors.businessImpact: Port delays, freight cost changes, and shipping schedule reliability can affect delivery performance in premium destination markets.
impactFactors.forecastUse: Apply scenario bands to destination exposure when logistics reliability weakens.

impactFactors.businessImpact: Export certification, biosecurity status, tariff access, and destination-market rules can quickly change achievable volume by country.
impactFactors.forecastUse: Use destination concentration insights to prioritise risk monitoring for key markets.

impactFactors.businessImpact: Exchange rates, retail demand, foodservice recovery, and competitor supply influence how much volume destinations can absorb.
impactFactors.forecastUse: Use the high case only where demand strength is supported by market pricing and destination growth signals.

recommendations: Use beef as the headline growth story, but frame it with concentration risk in the largest destination markets.
Position lamb as a market-portfolio problem rather than a simple decline story: identify growing destinations separately from falling ones.
Track export share of production as the core supply-demand pressure metric across the forecast year.
Connect the scenario bands to operating assumptions: climate affects supply, logistics affects executable shipments, and trade access affects destination allocation.
Add external datasets in a later version for rainfall, freight rates, exchange rates, and feed costs to turn the qualitative risk matrix into a quantified driver model.
limitations: Forecasts are based on the historical ABS and DAFF series currently packaged in the project.
Environmental, logistics, currency, and trade factors are represented as scenario drivers, not live external inputs.
The next enhancement would join external climate, freight, feed-cost, and FX datasets into the forecasting layer.

artifacts.label: Exports Clean
artifacts.description: Monthly destination-level export facts with subtotal rows removed.

artifacts.label: Exports Quarterly
artifacts.description: Quarterly destination-level export totals with completeness flags.

artifacts.description: Executive comparison layer for quarterly exports, production, and slaughter.

artifacts.description: Structured business insight table with narratives, signals, and recommendations.

artifacts.description: Scenario forecast output for exports, production, and export-share pressure.

qualityRules: Keep only ABS Original series and DAFF monthly_flow exports.
Deduplicate production by date + series_id using the latest release month.
Deduplicate exports by report_month + destination + metric_name using the latest release month.
Exclude subtotal destinations such as Total Asia, Other EU, and All Other Countries.
Limit the business reporting window to 2020-01 through 2025-12 after deduplication.
